data.name: Pinterest-native creative v5 forward test
data.objective: Forward-test the reusable product-faithful creative skill on a fresh verified product and identify which product-specific visual mechanism produces the most native, tasteful result.
data.hypothesis: A scene derived from an exact product feature will feel more intentional and saveable than a tasteful but interchangeable lifestyle setup.
successCriteria: At least two meaningfully different product-bound concepts pass every hard gate and average 4.0 or higher.
Every generated artifact uses a locally inspected exact product reference and a captured prompt.
The forward test yields one concrete reusable workflow improvement supported by observed evidence.
constraints: Do not publish, post, contact anyone, spend, subscribe, or prepare a public pilot.
Use the source image rather than catalog prose as authority when they disagree.
Preserve all attempts and internal review decisions.

data.statement: Literal source-image inspection prevented catalog copy from creating a false premise: B09TXJM23M is a printed T-shirt rather than sanitizer, while B0CBSXMFQV is an infant bib whose dense slogan is a weak beautiful-first hero.
data.decision: Use B0F9DZMQBL, whose exact mug form, personalized name, palette, and exaggerated hippo lips offer multiple inspectable product-specific visual mechanisms.
data.nextAction: Diverge into color-and-shape rhyme, category collision, and narrative placement before generating separate attempts.

data.title: Patricia hippo mug Memphis breakfast
data.audience: Pinterest users who save playful kitchen styling, colorful breakfast, and unusual mugs
data.hook: A polished color-study breakfast scene centers a black mug whose red-lipped hippo becomes the strange second read.

product.name: Custom Cartoon Hippo Coffee Mug with Name, Funny ... (ASIN B0F9DZMQBL)

references.lesson: Preserve the glossy black mug, turquoise-and-orange medallion, brown hippo with large red lips, and turquoise Patricia personalization.

notes: Product identity and source image were inspected before concepting.
The grapefruit circle and palette are product-specific visual rhymes.

data.rationale: The verified product, product-specific circular/color rhyme, scale, audience, and anti-ad constraints are explicit.
Generate one product-bound artifact with internal generation authorized and all distribution prohibited.

data.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: vertical 2:3 Pinterest-native editorial still life
Primary request: Create a beautiful, playful Memphis-inspired breakfast still life built specifically around the exact mug in Reference 1.
Input images: Reference 1 is the strict product-identity reference. Preserve the ordinary large glossy black ceramic mug, its left-side handle when front-facing, the circular turquoise-and-orange medallion, the brown hippo with extremely large glossy red lips, and the turquoise personalized name “Patricia” below it. Keep the design printed flat on the ceramic; do not turn the hippo into a sculpture, animal, or separate object.
Scene/backdrop: a sophisticated sunlit breakfast table with a pale butter-yellow linen, a slim chrome vase, one halved pink grapefruit, and a small turquoise saucer used only as a color echo.
Composition/framing: vertical 2:3, close editorial crop, mug is the clear focal object at ordinary usable scale, three-quarter front view so the handle, hippo artwork, and name remain inspectable. Use the grapefruit circle as a subtle compositional rhyme, not a product collage.
Lighting/mood: directional warm morning window light, crisp but natural shadows, tactile analog editorial photography, restrained 1970s design-magazine energy.
Color palette: black, turquoise, tomato red, grapefruit pink, butter yellow, small chrome highlights.
Constraints: one immediately legible visual idea; beautiful first and strange on the double take; exact product fidelity; physically credible contact and scale; no people or hands.
Avoid: extra mugs, changed name, misspelled lettering, invented logos, packaging, price, CTA, badges, border, text outside the product’s real printed design, product-card framing, excessive props, glossy generic AI advertising finish, floating objects, malformed handle.
notes: Generated from the captured prompt with the exact local product reference supplied to image_gen.

data.rationale: The complete full-resolution artifact is ready for product-fidelity and taste review.

notes: The product silhouette, handle, black ceramic, medallion palette, red-lipped hippo, bridge background, and Patricia name remain inspectable.
The circular grapefruit and turquoise ceramics create a product-specific composition rather than generic lifestyle dressing.
The crop reads as colorful editorial breakfast styling without a CTA, frame, price, or promotional copy.

data.rationale: Every hard gate passes and the product-specific color-and-shape rhyme makes the execution distinctive and native.

data.title: Patricia hippo mug lipstick vanity
data.audience: Pinterest users who save maximalist vanity styling, beauty still lifes, and personalized gifts
data.hook: An elegant vintage vanity contains one black coffee mug whose exaggerated red-lipped hippo looks unexpectedly at home among lipsticks.

product.name: Custom Cartoon Hippo Coffee Mug with Name, Funny ... (ASIN B0F9DZMQBL)

references.lesson: The exaggerated glossy red lips motivate a beauty-category collision while the mug, art, and name must remain unchanged.

notes: The scene mechanism is derived from the product's most distinctive feature, not a reusable room template.

data.rationale: The verified product, red-lip category collision, mirror risk, scale, and anti-ad constraints are explicit.
Generate one product-bound artifact with internal generation authorized and all distribution prohibited.

data.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: vertical 2:3 Pinterest-native editorial beauty still life
Primary request: Create an elegant vintage vanity scene whose only absurd element is the exact personalized hippo mug in Reference 1.
Input images: Reference 1 is the strict product-identity reference. Preserve the ordinary large glossy black ceramic mug, its left-side handle when front-facing, the circular turquoise-and-orange medallion, the brown hippo with extremely large glossy red lips, and the turquoise personalized name “Patricia” below it. Keep the hippo image as a flat printed design on the mug.
Scene/backdrop: a dark walnut vanity with a softly aged oval mirror, one uncapped classic red lipstick, a small amber perfume bottle, and a folded ivory linen. The mug sits naturally among the objects as though Patricia uses it while getting ready.
Composition/framing: vertical 2:3, intimate three-quarter editorial crop, exactly one mug, front art and name fully inspectable, ordinary coffee-mug scale. Keep mirror reflections soft and abstract so they do not duplicate the mug or create another face.
Lighting/mood: low warm side light like a refined independent interiors magazine, real ceramic gloss, subtle film grain, rich but restrained shadows.
Color palette: black lacquer, oxblood red, turquoise, dark walnut, amber, ivory.
Constraints: beauty first, humorous category collision on second read; one clear idea; exact product identity; physically believable contact; no people or hands.
Avoid: extra mugs, changed or garbled name, altered hippo lips, humanlike hippo, lipstick applied to the mug, packaging, logos, added copy, CTA, price, badges, borders, product-card composition, generic luxury-ad gloss, impossible reflection, malformed handle, floating props.
notes: Generated from the captured prompt with the exact local product reference supplied to image_gen.

data.rationale: The complete full-resolution artifact is ready for product-fidelity and taste review.

notes: The mug's verified silhouette, print, handle, scale, and Patricia name remain clear, and the mirror does not duplicate it.
The lipstick creates an immediate product-specific visual joke without applying new marks to the product.
The dark styling is distinctive and editorial, though slightly less broadly saveable than the bright breakfast scene.

data.rationale: Every hard gate passes and the lip-driven category collision is specific, tasteful, and immediately legible.

data.title: Patricia hippo mug creative-director desk
data.audience: Pinterest users who save stylish desk setups, creative offices, and funny coworker gifts
data.hook: A disciplined creative workspace gives the personalized hippo mug the visual authority of the person running the meeting.

product.name: Custom Cartoon Hippo Coffee Mug with Name, Funny ... (ASIN B0F9DZMQBL)

references.lesson: Keep the personalized front readable from a high-oblique angle and use the exact turquoise/red palette sparingly in work props.

notes: This direction tests narrative placement against the more directly product-derived visual mechanisms.

data.rationale: The product, narrative placement, high-oblique fidelity risk, prop restraint, and anti-ad constraints are explicit.
Generate one product-bound artifact with internal generation authorized and all distribution prohibited.

data.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: vertical 2:3 Pinterest-native editorial desk photograph
Primary request: Create a disciplined, genuinely useful creative-director desk setup in which the exact personalized hippo mug from Reference 1 quietly appears to be running the meeting.
Input images: Reference 1 is the strict product-identity reference. Preserve the ordinary large glossy black ceramic mug, the circular turquoise-and-orange medallion, the brown hippo with extremely large glossy red lips, and the turquoise personalized name “Patricia” below it. Preserve an ordinary ceramic handle and keep the art printed flat on the front.
Scene/backdrop: an editorial design studio desk with a gridded notebook, black fountain pen, two clipped paper color swatches in turquoise and red, one small architectural desk lamp, and the edge of a closed silver laptop. No writing or legible labels on any prop.
Composition/framing: vertical 2:3, high oblique overhead view rather than straight top-down, strong graphic negative space, exactly one mug placed at the head of a subtle semicircle of work objects. Angle the mug so the front art, “Patricia,” and handle remain fully inspectable at believable coffee-mug scale.
Lighting/mood: cool north-window daylight with a narrow warm lamp accent, clean real editorial photography, mild paper texture and ceramic reflections, confident not cute.
Color palette: off-white paper, charcoal, black, restrained turquoise and red accents.
Constraints: one clear narrative idea; plausible saved desk-styling post first, ridiculous product moment second; exact product fidelity and physical placement; no people or hands.
Avoid: extra mugs, oversized product, changed or garbled name, altered hippo face, flat-lay e-commerce grid, labels, added copy, logo, packaging, CTA, price, badges, borders, ad-template structure, generic AI luxury gloss, impossible handle, floating stationery, malformed laptop.
notes: Generated from the captured prompt with the exact local product reference supplied to image_gen.

data.rationale: The complete full-resolution artifact is ready for product-fidelity and taste review.

notes: The product remains unusually legible despite the high-oblique composition, with correct name, handle, front art, material, and scale.
The desk is useful and board-native, but the narrative that Patricia is running the meeting is less immediate than the breakfast and lipstick visual mechanisms.
This is the most interchangeable scene of the three even though its palette and personalization remain product-bound.

data.rationale: Every hard gate passes and the setup is highly saveable, though it ranks third because its hook is less product-specific.

data.statement: All three source-guided attempts were truthful and tasteful, but the two strongest hooks were derived directly from the product's exact visual features: the medallion palette and circle in the breakfast scene, and the exaggerated red lips in the vanity scene. The generic desk context remained attractive but required more explanation.
data.decision: Add a product-derived-hook test to concept selection: require the chosen scene to name the exact product feature that makes this execution possible and reject concepts that would work unchanged after swapping in an unrelated product.
data.nextAction: Show the three internal survivors to the owner in ranked order; keep all public distribution prohibited and apply the product-derived-hook test in the next independent-product forward test.
